cards.front: Why are units used to express the result of a measurement?
cards.back: Units are used to express the result of a measurement because they provide a reference standard for comparison and help to ensure accuracy and consistency.
cards.question: Why are units used to express the result of a measurement?
cards.answer: Units are used to express the result of a measurement because they provide a reference standard for comparison and help to ensure accuracy and consistency.

cards.front: What are the units of the fundamental or base quantities called?
cards.back: fundamental or base units
cards.question: What are the units of the fundamental or base quantities called?
cards.answer: fundamental or base units

cards.front: What are the fundamental or base units?
cards.back: They are the units that cannot be expressed as a combination of other units.
cards.question: What are the fundamental or base units?
cards.answer: They are the units that cannot be expressed as a combination of other units.

cards.front: What is the primary purpose of using units in measurement?
cards.back: The primary purpose of using units in measurement is to compare a physical quantity with a certain basic, arbitrarily chosen, internationally accepted reference standard called a unit.
cards.question: What is the primary purpose of using units in measurement?
cards.answer: The primary purpose of using units in measurement is to compare a physical quantity with a certain basic, arbitrarily chosen, internationally accepted reference standard called a unit.

cards.front: Why are there only a limited number of units needed to express all physical quantities?
cards.back: Physical quantities are inter-related with one another, so only a limited number of units are necessary.
cards.question: Why are there only a limited number of units needed to express all physical quantities?
cards.answer: Physical quantities are inter-related with one another, so only a limited number of units are necessary.

cards.front: Why are only a limited number of units needed to express all physical quantities?
cards.back: All physical quantities are inter-related with one another, so only a limited number of base units are needed.
cards.question: Why are only a limited number of units needed to express all physical quantities?
cards.answer: All physical quantities are inter-related with one another, so only a limited number of base units are needed.

cards.front: In what year was the International System of Units (SI) developed by the Bureau International des Poids et mesures (BIPM)?
cards.question: In what year was the International System of Units (SI) developed by the Bureau International des Poids et mesures (BIPM)?

cards.front: What are the three fundamental units of the International System of Units (SI)?
cards.back: metre, kilogram and second
cards.question: What are the three fundamental units of the International System of Units (SI)?
cards.answer: metre, kilogram and second

cards.front: Which of the three fundamental units in the SI system is defined as the unit of time?
cards.back: Second
cards.question: Which of the three fundamental units in the SI system is defined as the unit of time?
cards.answer: Second

cards.front: What are the three basic units in the International System of Units (SI)?
cards.back: metre, kilogram and second respectively
cards.question: What are the three basic units in the International System of Units (SI)?
cards.answer: metre, kilogram and second respectively

cards.front: What are the fundamental units for measurement?
cards.back: The fundamental or base units are used for expressing all physical quantities.
cards.question: What are the fundamental units for measurement?
cards.answer: The fundamental or base units are used for expressing all physical quantities.

cards.front: What are the units of all other physical quantities called?
cards.back: derived units
cards.question: What are the units of all other physical quantities called?
cards.answer: derived units

cards.back: Because the number of physical quantities is very large, but we only need a limited number of units for expressing all physical quantities
cards.question: Why are base units needed?
cards.answer: Because the number of physical quantities is very large, but we only need a limited number of units for expressing all physical quantities

cards.front: Why do we need only a limited number of base units to express all physical quantities?
cards.back: All physical quantities are inter-related with one another.
cards.question: Why do we need only a limited number of base units to express all physical quantities?
cards.answer: All physical quantities are inter-related with one another.

cards.front: What are the seven base units in the SI system?
cards.back: Meter (m), Kilogram (kg), Second (s), Kelvin (K), Ampere (A), Mole (mol), and Candela (cd)
cards.question: What are the seven base units in the SI system?
cards.answer: Meter (m), Kilogram (kg), Second (s), Kelvin (K), Ampere (A), Mole (mol), and Candela (cd)

cards.front: What are the seven base units in the International System of Units (SI) and how are they used?
cards.back: The seven base units in SI are metre (m), kilogram (kg), second (s), ampere (A), Kelvin (K), mole (mol), and candela (cd). These units are used to describe various physical quantities.
cards.question: What are the seven base units in the International System of Units (SI) and how are they used?
cards.answer: The seven base units in SI are metre (m), kilogram (kg), second (s), ampere (A), Kelvin (K), mole (mol), and candela (cd). These units are used to describe various physical quantities.

cards.front: Why are base units necessary for expressing all physical quantities?
cards.back: Because they are the fundamental or base quantities, and the units of all other physical quantities can be expressed as combinations of them.
cards.question: Why are base units necessary for expressing all physical quantities?
cards.answer: Because they are the fundamental or base quantities, and the units of all other physical quantities can be expressed as combinations of them.

cards.front: What causes the result of a measurement to be expressed by a number accompanied by a unit?
cards.back: Measurement of a physical quantity involves comparison with a certain basic, arbitrarily chosen, internationally accepted reference standard called a unit.
cards.question: What causes the result of a measurement to be expressed by a number accompanied by a unit?
cards.answer: Measurement of a physical quantity involves comparison with a certain basic, arbitrarily chosen, internationally accepted reference standard called a unit.

cards.front: What are the three main systems of units that were used extensively till recently?
cards.back: The CGS, FPS (or British) system, and the MKS system
cards.question: What are the three main systems of units that were used extensively till recently?
cards.answer: The CGS, FPS (or British) system, and the MKS system

cards.front: What were the base units for length, mass, and time in the CGS system?
cards.back: centimetre, gram, and second respectively.
cards.question: What were the base units for length, mass, and time in the CGS system?
cards.answer: centimetre, gram, and second respectively.

cards.front: What causes the need for a limited number of units for measuring physical quantities?
cards.back: Physical quantities are inter-related with one another, making only a limited number of units necessary for expressing all physical quantities.
cards.question: What causes the need for a limited number of units for measuring physical quantities?
cards.answer: Physical quantities are inter-related with one another, making only a limited number of units necessary for expressing all physical quantities.

cards.front: What is the name of the system of units that is internationally accepted for measurement?
cards.back: The Système Internationale d'Unites (SI), abbreviated as SI.
cards.question: What is the name of the system of units that is internationally accepted for measurement?
cards.answer: The Système Internationale d'Unites (SI), abbreviated as SI.

cards.front: How do the base units for length, mass, and time differ in the CGS, FPS, and MKS systems?
cards.back: The base units are: centimetre, gram, and second for CGS; foot, pound, and second for FPS; and metre, kilogram, and second for MKS.
cards.question: How do the base units for length, mass, and time differ in the CGS, FPS, and MKS systems?
cards.answer: The base units are: centimetre, gram, and second for CGS; foot, pound, and second for FPS; and metre, kilogram, and second for MKS.

cards.front: What is the result of a measurement of a physical quantity expressed as?
cards.back: A number (or numerical measure) accompanied by a unit.
cards.question: What is the result of a measurement of a physical quantity expressed as?
cards.answer: A number (or numerical measure) accompanied by a unit.

cards.front: What is the term for the units obtained for the derived quantities that can be expressed as combinations of the base units?
cards.back: Derived units.
cards.question: What is the term for the units obtained for the derived quantities that can be expressed as combinations of the base units?
cards.answer: Derived units.

cards.front: Who developed the standard scheme of symbols, units, and abbreviations for the SI system?
cards.back: The Bureau International des Poids et mesures (BIPM).
cards.question: Who developed the standard scheme of symbols, units, and abbreviations for the SI system?
cards.answer: The Bureau International des Poids et mesures (BIPM).

cards.front: When was the SI system developed?
cards.question: When was the SI system developed?

cards.front: What is the abbreviation for the French name of the International System of Units?
cards.back: Système Internationale d'Unites (SI).
cards.question: What is the abbreviation for the French name of the International System of Units?
cards.answer: Système Internationale d'Unites (SI).

cards.front: How was the SI system revised recently?
cards.back: Recently revised by the General Conference on Weights and Measures in November 2018.
cards.question: How was the SI system revised recently?
cards.answer: Recently revised by the General Conference on Weights and Measures in November 2018.

cards.front: What is the main purpose of a system of units?
cards.back: To provide a complete set of units, both base units and derived units, for expressing all physical quantities.
cards.question: What is the main purpose of a system of units?
cards.answer: To provide a complete set of units, both base units and derived units, for expressing all physical quantities.
